points.text: Acumen Pharmaceuticals — Bispecific antibodies targeting Aβ oligomers and Transferrin Receptors — Alzheimer's Disease — Preclinical data presented at AAIC 2026 demonstrated enhanced brain delivery in Cynomolgus monkeys via EBD technology.

Syndax Pharmaceuticals — Niktimvo (axatilimab-csfr) — FDA-approved monoclonal antibody blocking CSF-1 receptor for cancer therapy — Commercial-stage asset advancing alongside Revuforj (revumenib) menin inhibitor.

Spero Therapeutics / Innovent Biologics — SP001 (IBI355) — Third-generation Fc-silent anti-CD40L antibody — Exclusive global license (excluding Greater China) secured with $1.1B potential milestones; Phase 2 trials planned for IgG4-RD and Sjögren's disease.

Spero Therapeutics secured $105M non-recourse financing to advance SP001 (IBI355), a Phase 2-ready Fc-silent third-generation anti-CD40L monoclonal antibody, following an exclusive license with Innovent Biologics.
AbbVie agreed to acquire Apogee Therapeutics for $10.9B, gaining control of zumilokibart, a monoclonal antibody for atopic dermatitis, asthma, and eosinophilic esophagitis.
Incyte announced a $1.25B acquisition of Star Therapeutics to secure VGA039, a monoclonal antibody for von Willebrand disease currently in Phase 3 trials.
GSK expanded its oncology pipeline via a $10.6B acquisition of Nuvalent and previously acquired RAPT Therapeutics, a monoclonal antibody developer.
Sanofi received FDA approval for Sarclisa (isatuximab-irfc) Escena, a subcutaneous monoclonal antibody formulation for multiple myeloma, administered via the CirCLIQ on-body injector.

Samsung Biologics published a platform strategy report on improving manufacturability and efficacy in bispecific antibodies, addressing structural complexities like chain mispairing and product heterogeneity.
Bristol-Myers Squibb received FDA NDA acceptance for mezigdomide + carfilzomib + dexamethasone (MeziKd) for relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma, with PDUFA date set for May 13, 2027.
Polpharma and Teva signed an exclusive licensing deal for a biosimilar candidate to Ocrevus (ocrelizumab), a humanized monoclonal antibody targeting CD20-positive B cells for multiple sclerosis.

The SUCCESSOR-2 Phase 3 trial demonstrated a 52% reduction in risk of disease progression or death for MeziKd in patients with relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma, including those previously treated with an anti-CD38 monoclonal antibody.

Acumen Pharmaceuticals — Bispecific antibodies (AβO + TfR) — Alzheimer's Disease — Oral presentation at AAIC 2026 detailed preclinical data showing enhanced brain delivery in Cynomolgus monkeys using Enhanced Brain Delivery (EBD) technology.
Syndax Pharmaceuticals — Niktimvo (axatilimab-csfr) — Cancer (CSF-1R blockade) — FDA-approved monoclonal antibody currently in commercial stage.

Spero Therapeutics / Innovent Biologics — SP001 (IBI355) — IgG4-related disease / Sjögren's disease — Exclusive license agreement signed; Spero to initiate Phase 2 in IgG4-RD Q2 2027, Innovent to initiate Phase 2 in SjD early 2027.

Spero Therapeutics — SP001 (IBI355) — Immune-mediated diseases — In-licensed from Innovent Biologics; Phase 2-ready Fc-silent third-generation anti-CD40L monoclonal antibody; $105M financing secured for development.
AbbVie — Zumilokibart — Atopic dermatitis, asthma, eosinophilic esophagitis — Acquired via $10.9B takeover of Apogee Therapeutics; monoclonal antibody asset transferred to AbbVie portfolio.
Incyte — VGA039 — von Willebrand disease — Acquired via $1.25B deal with Star Therapeutics; Phase 3 monoclonal antibody with FDA fast track, orphan drug, and breakthrough therapy designations.

Sanofi — Sarclisa (isatuximab-irfc) Escena — Multiple Myeloma — FDA approved subcutaneous formulation administered via CirCLIQ on-body injector; supported by IRAKLIA Phase 3 non-inferiority study showing comparable efficacy and safety to IV infusion with reduced treatment time.

Samsung Biologics — Bispecific Antibody Platform — Oncology — Developed molecular engineering strategies to optimize heavy- and light-chain assembly, improving yield, stability, and manufacturability while maintaining target binding in preclinical models.

Bristol-Myers Squibb — Mezigdomide + Carfilzomib + Dexamethasone (MeziKd) — Relapsed/Refractory Multiple Myeloma — FDA NDA accepted; PDUFA date May 13, 2027; Phase 3 SUCCESSOR-2 trial showed 52% PFS reduction vs control.

Polpharma / Teva — Ocrelizumab Biosimilar — Multiple Sclerosis — Exclusive licensing deal signed for IV and SC formulations; Polpharma leads manufacturing, Teva handles commercialization in US, EU, and other key markets.

Acumen Pharmaceuticals — Enhanced Brain Delivery (EBD) technology commercialization — Bispecific antibodies targeting Aβ oligomers — Collaboration with JCR Pharmaceuticals ongoing; preclinical data presented at AAIC 2026.
Syndax Pharmaceuticals — Commercial-stage oncology expansion — Niktimvo (axatilimab-csfr, anti-CSF-1R mAb) + Revuforj (revumenib) — FDA-approved assets driving commercial operations.
Spero Therapeutics — Immune-mediated disease pipeline build-out — SP001 (anti-CD40L mAb) — In-licensed from Innovent; $105M non-recourse financing secured to support development.
Spero Therapeutics — Immunology pipeline expansion — SP001 (anti-CD40L mAb) + Utebzi — $105M non-dilutive financing secured; cash runway extended to H2 2029.
AbbVie — Monoclonal antibody portfolio growth — Zumilokibart (via Apogee) — $10.9B acquisition; adds asset for atopic dermatitis and eosinophilic esophagitis to existing Humira/Skyrizi franchise.
Incyte — Bleeding disorders expansion — VGA039 (monoclonal antibody) — $1.25B acquisition of Star Therapeutics; targets von Willebrand disease market with Phase 3 candidate.

Sanofi — Subcutaneous monoclonal antibody commercialization — Sarclisa (isatuximab-irfc) Escena — FDA approval secured for first anticancer treatment via on-body injector; over 70,000 patients treated globally with IV formulation.

Samsung Biologics — Bispecific antibody manufacturing optimization — Platform technology for complex biologics — Published strategy to reduce downstream purification burden and enhance scalability for next-generation targeted therapies.

Bristol-Myers Squibb — NDA submission for next-generation immunomodulator — Mezigdomide (MeziKd) — Phase 3 SUCCESSOR-2 data supports filing; includes patients with prior anti-CD38 monoclonal antibody exposure.

Polpharma / Teva — Biosimilar commercialization strategy — Ocrelizumab biosimilar — Exclusive rights granted for global commercialization of CD20-targeting monoclonal antibody biosimilar for multiple sclerosis.
Innovent Biologics — Global licensing of SP001 — SP001 (IBI355) anti-CD40L monoclonal antibody — Received upfront payment; eligible for up to $1.1B in milestones and tiered royalties.
GSK — Oncology and mAb pipeline diversification — zidesamtinib + RAPT Therapeutics assets — $10.6B Nuvalent deal plus acquisition of monoclonal antibody developer RAPT.

Bispecific antibodies (AβO + TfR) — Mechanism: Dual binding to amyloid beta oligomers and Transferrin Receptors for enhanced CNS transport — Phase: Preclinical — Population: Cynomolgus monkeys — Comparator: N/A — Endpoints/Results: Demonstrated enhanced brain delivery via EBD technology.

Niktimvo (axatilimab-csfr) — Mechanism: Monoclonal antibody blocking colony stimulating factor 1 (CSF-1) receptor — Phase: Commercial (FDA-approved) — Population: Cancer patients — Status: Approved therapy.

SP001 (IBI355) — Mechanism: Third-generation Fc-silent anti-CD40L antibody — Phase: Pre-Phase 2 (planned Q2 2027) — Population: IgG4-related disease (IgG4-RD) and Sjögren's disease — Status: In-licensed for global development.

SP001 (IBI355) — Mechanism: Fc-silent third-generation anti-CD40L monoclonal antibody — Phase: 2-ready — Population: Immune-mediated diseases — Status: In-licensed from Innovent Biologics; development funded by $105M royalty financing.

VGA039 — Mechanism: Monoclonal antibody targeting von Willebrand disease — Phase: 3 — Population: Patients with inherited bleeding disorder (approx. 1% of population) — Regulatory: FDA fast track, orphan drug, rare pediatric disease, breakthrough therapy designations.

Zumilokibart — Mechanism: Monoclonal antibody — Phase: Pre-commercial (acquired) — Population: Atopic dermatitis, asthma, eosinophilic esophagitis — Status: Acquired by AbbVie for $10.9B via Apogee Therapeutics.

Sarclisa (isatuximab-irfc) — Mechanism: Anti-CD38 monoclonal antibody — Phase: 3 (IRAKLIA) — Population: Multiple myeloma patients — Comparator: Intravenous infusion — Results: Non-inferior efficacy, pharmacokinetics, and safety; significantly shorter treatment time and fewer infusion-related reactions with subcutaneous on-body injector delivery.

Bispecific Antibodies — Mechanism: Dual-target engagement for complex disease modulation — Phase: Preclinical/Development — Population: Tumor models — Comparator: N/A — Results: Engineered designs achieved high yields and robust manufacturability without compromising antitumor activity or target binding.

Mezigdomide — Mechanism: Cereblon E3 ligase modulator (CELMoD) — Phase: 3 (SUCCESSOR-2) — Population: Relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma (including post anti-CD38 monoclonal antibody) — Comparator: Carfilzomib + dexamethasone — Results: 52% reduction in risk of progression/death; 479 patients randomized.

Ocrelizumab Biosimilar — Mechanism: Humanized monoclonal antibody targeting CD20-positive B cells — Phase: Pre-approval (biosimilar development) — Population: Multiple sclerosis — Comparator: Reference product Ocrevus — Status: Exclusive licensing deal signed for IV and SC formulations.

Spero Therapeutics / Innovent Biologics — SP001 (IBI355) license — Exclusive global rights (excl. Greater China) — Upfront payment + $1.1B total milestones + tiered royalties; Spero secured $105M non-recourse financing to fund development.
Spero Therapeutics / Healthcare Royalty — $105M non-recourse non-dilutive financing — Secured against Utebzi milestones/royalties to fund SP001 (anti-CD40L mAb) Phase 2 development.
AbbVie / Apogee Therapeutics — $10.9B acquisition — Transfers zumilokibart (monoclonal antibody) for atopic dermatitis, asthma, and eosinophilic esophagitis to AbbVie.
Incyte / Star Therapeutics — $1.25B acquisition — Acquires VGA039 (monoclonal antibody for von Willebrand disease); deal includes up to $750M in sales milestones.
GSK / RAPT Therapeutics — Acquisition of monoclonal antibody developer — Part of GSK's broader biotech M&A activity in June 2026 alongside $10.6B Nuvalent purchase.

Sanofi — Commercial launch of subcutaneous monoclonal antibody — Sarclisa Escena — First anticancer treatment approved for administration via on-body injector (CirCLIQ) in the US, leveraging Enable Injections' enFuse platform.

Syndax Pharmaceuticals — Pipeline expansion strategy — Niktimvo (axatilimab-csfr) monoclonal antibody + SNDX-4321 (EGFR inhibitor) + SNDX-62122 — Unveiling new assets at R&D event while advancing late-stage programs.

Polpharma / Teva — Exclusive licensing agreement — Ocrelizumab biosimilar — Polpharma retains development/manufacturing; Teva secures commercialization rights across US, EU, Brazil, Canada, Australia, New Zealand, Israel, and Turkey.
